title: meconopsis aprica
intro: Meconopsis aprica, also known as the Himalayan blue poppy or Tibetan poppy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and heal wounds. Meconopsis aprica is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's dried roots and flowers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
